terms.definition: The relationship created between a CAD structure and a Windchill part structure. It enables the exchange and update of structure, attribute, and visualization information between them.

An action that automatically locates and associates an existing Windchill part to a CAD document, or creates a new Windchill part and associates it to a CAD document if no matching part currently exists.
A snapshot of an evolving collection of Windchill data objects and their status at a specific point in time. Each baseline contains a static list of objects that can be locked and protected.
A comprehensive inventory of the raw materials, assemblies, subassemblies, parts, components, and the quantities of each needed to manufacture a product. Can be single-level or multi-level.
A CAD-driven approach where the design process begins in the CAD authoring environment. CAD assembly structures are checked into Windchill to generate an analogous enterprise part structure.
A Windchill object that represents a CAD model assembly checked into Windchill. It can be a part, drawing, or assembly and preserves the membership-in-assembly relationships.
A version-to-version link between a Windchill part and a document. Used when a specific version of a part is described by only one version of the document, such as a drawing.
The real-time synchronization of eBOM, mBOM, and sBOM structures that critically informs a single source of truth defining the product across engineering, manufacturing, and service departments.
Defines the design of the finished product. Includes all alternative and substitute part numbers, product codes, part names, numbers, revisions, descriptions, quantities, and specifications.
A special type of Windchill part that typically represents the top node of a product structure. End items are developed for delivery and may also appear throughout the product structure.
Comprises all assemblies and parts required to construct a finished item, including packaging materials and manufacturing process information.
Stores information about one specific named use of a component within an assembly Windchill part structure. The number of occurrences relates to the quantity of parts used in the parent assembly.

A central location in Windchill for accessing information related to a part and performing actions. Includes tabs for Details, Structure, Related Objects, Changes, History, Where Used, and Traceability.
A hierarchical representation of Windchill parts that makes up a specific product, capturing all product configurations from first release through current production to future releases.
The Structure tab on the Information page of a Windchill part. Used to create, edit, navigate, and filter product structures in both CAD-centric and part-centric environments.
A link set when the associated document type is Reference Document. The part may be revised many times, but the referenced document is always the latest released version.
An attribute attached to BOM components when more than one piece of the same component is used. Each occurrence stores unique information identified by its reference designator name.
Represents a finished product assembled at the sales stage. Includes the finished product and other components that appear as separate items in the sales order document.
A tool that reduces the complexity of a part structure by showing valid product configurations for a point in time, serial number, or specific development task.
A part-driven approach where Windchill parts and structures are created first, independent of CAD. Tasks of designing and linking CAD models are then distributed to engineering teams.
Attributes defined on a usage link between a selected child part and the next higher assembly, including Quantity, Unit, Trace Code, Line Number, Find Number, and Reference Designator.
A link between a Windchill part and a WTPartMaster used to build assemblies. The usage link selects the correct version of the WTPartMaster for the product structure.

A logical database representation of a physical item used to build a product. Parts organize product information through relationships and enable associated data such as design files, attributes, documents, and changes.
